fields.name: TAMP1MSK
fields.description: Tamper 1 mask The tamper 1 interrupt must not be enabled when TAMP1MSK is set.

fields.name: TAMP2MSK
fields.description: Tamper 2 mask The tamper 2 interrupt must not be enabled when TAMP2MSK is set.

fields.name: TAMP3MSK
fields.description: Tamper 3 mask The tamper 3 interrupt must not be enabled when TAMP3MSK is set.

fields.name: BKBLOCK
fields.description: Backup registers and device secrets access blocked

fields.name: BKERASE
fields.description: Backup registers and device secrets erase Writing '1' to this bit reset the backup registers and device secrets(1). Writing 0 has no effect. This bit is always read as 0.

fields.name: TAMP1TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 1 input If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 1 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 1 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP2TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 2 input If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 2 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 2 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP3TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 3 input If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 3 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 3 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP4TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 4 input (active mode disabled) If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 4 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 4 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP5TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 5 input (active mode disabled) If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 5 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 5 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP6TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 6 input (active mode disabled) If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 6 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 6 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP7TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 7 input (active mode disabled) If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 7 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 7 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMP8TRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 8 input (active mode disabled) If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 8 input rising edge triggers a tamper detection event. If TAMPFLT = 00 Tamper 8 input falling edge triggers a tamper detection event.

fields.name: TAMPFREQ
fields.description: Tamper sampling frequency Determines the frequency at which each of the TAMP_INx inputs are sampled.

fields.name: TAMPFLT
fields.description: TAMP_INx filter count These bits determines the number of consecutive samples at the specified level (TAMP*TRG) needed to activate a tamper event. TAMPFLT is valid for each of the TAMP_INx inputs.

fields.name: TAMPPRCH
fields.description: TAMP_INx precharge duration These bit determines the duration of time during which the pull-up/is activated before each sample. TAMPPRCH is valid for each of the TAMP_INx inputs.

fields.name: TAMPPUDIS
fields.description: TAMP_INx pull-up disable This bit determines if each of the TAMPx pins are precharged before each sample.

fields.name: TAMP1AM
fields.description: Tamper 1 active mode

fields.name: TAMP2AM
fields.description: Tamper 2 active mode

fields.name: TAMP3AM
fields.description: Tamper 3 active mode

fields.name: TAMP4AM
fields.description: Tamper 4 active mode

fields.name: ATOSEL1
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 1 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout

fields.name: ATOSEL2
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 2 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout

fields.name: ATOSEL3
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 3 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout

fields.name: ATOSEL4
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 4 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout.

fields.name: ATCKSEL
fields.description: Active tamper RTC asynchronous prescaler clock selection These bits selects the RTC asynchronous prescaler stage output.The selected clock is CK_ATPRE. fCK_ATPRE = fRTCCLK / 2ATCKSEL when (PREDIV_A+1) = 128. ... These bits can be written only when all active tampers are disabled. The write protection remains for up to 1.5 ck_atpre cycles after all the active tampers are disable.

fields.name: ATPER
fields.description: Active tamper output change period The tamper output is changed every CK_ATPER = (2ATPER x CK_ATPRE) cycles. Refer to .

fields.name: ATOSHARE
fields.description: Active tamper output sharing TAMP_IN1 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL1 TAMP_IN2 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL2 TAMP_IN3 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL3 TAMP_IN4 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL4 TAMP_IN5 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL5 TAMP_IN6 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL6 TAMP_IN7 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL7 TAMP_IN8 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL8

fields.name: FLTEN
fields.description: Active tamper filter enable

fields.name: SEED
fields.description: Pseudo-random generator seed value This register must be written four times with 32-bit values to provide the 128-bit seed to the PRNG. Writing to this register automatically sends the seed value to the PRNG.

fields.name: PRNG
fields.description: Pseudo-random generator value This field provides the values of the PRNG output. Because of potential inconsistencies due to synchronization delays, PRNG must be read at least twice. The read value is correct if it is equal to previous read value. This field can only be read when the APB is in secure mode.

fields.name: SEEDF
fields.description: Seed running flag This flag is set by hardware when a new seed is written in the TAMP_ATSEEDR. It is cleared by hardware when the PRNG has absorbed this new seed, and by system reset. The TAMP APB cock must not be switched off as long as SEEDF is set.

fields.name: INITS
fields.description: Active tamper initialization status This flag is set by hardware when the PRNG has absorbed the first 128-bit seed, meaning that the enabled active tampers are functional. This flag is cleared when the active tampers are disabled.

fields.name: ATOSEL1
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 1 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout. Bits 9:8 are the mirror of ATOSEL1[1:0] in the TAMP_ATCR1, and so can also be read or written through TAMP_ATCR1.

fields.name: ATOSEL2
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 2 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout. Bits 12:11 are the mirror of ATOSEL2[1:0] in the TAMP_ATCR1, and so can also be read or written through TAMP_ATCR1.

fields.name: ATOSEL3
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 3 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout. Bits 15:14 are the mirror of ATOSEL3[1:0] in the TAMP_ATCR1, and so can also be read or written through TAMP_ATCR1.

fields.name: ATOSEL4
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 4 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout. Bits 18:17 are the mirror of ATOSEL2[1:0] in the TAMP_ATCR1, and so can also be read or written through TAMP_ATCR1.

fields.name: ATOSEL5
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 5 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout.

fields.name: ATOSEL6
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 6 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout.

fields.name: ATOSEL7
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 7 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout.

fields.name: ATOSEL8
fields.description: Active tamper shared output 8 selection The selected output must be available in the package pinout.

fields.name: BKPRWSEC
fields.description: Backup registers read/write protection offset Protection zone 1 is defined for backup registers from TAMP_BKP0R to TAMP_BKPxR (x = BKPRWSEC-1, from 0 to 128). if TZEN=1, these backup registers can be read and written only with secure access. If TZEN=0: the protection zone 1 can be read and written with non-secure access. If BKPRWSEC = 0: there is no protection zone 1. If BKPRWPRIV is set, BKPRWSEC[7:0] can be written only in privileged mode.

fields.name: CNT1SEC
fields.description: Monotonic counter 1 secure protection

fields.name: BKPWSEC
fields.description: Backup registers write protection offset Protection zone 2 is defined for backup registers from TAMP_BKPyR (y = BKPRWSEC, from 0 to 128) to TAMP_BKPzR (z = BKPWSEC-1, from 0 to 128, BKPWSEC greater than or equal BKPRWSEC): if TZEN=1, these backup registers can be written only with secure access. They can be read with secure or non-secure access. Protection zone 3 defined for backup registers from TAMP_BKPtR (t = BKPWSEC, from 0 to 127). They can be read or written with secure or non-secure access. If TZEN=0: the protection zone 2 can be read and written with non-secure access. If BKPWSEC = 0 or if BKPWSEC less than or equal BKPRWSEC: there is no protection zone 2. If BKPWPRIV is set, BKPRWSEC[7:0] can be written only in privileged mode.

fields.name: BHKLOCK
fields.description: Boot hardware key lock This bit can be read and can only be written to 1 by software. It is cleared by hardware together with the backup registers following a tamper detection event or when the readout protection (RDP) is disabled.

fields.name: TAMPSEC
fields.description: Tamper protection (excluding monotonic counters and backup registers) Note: Refer to for details on the read protection.

fields.name: CNT1PRIV
fields.description: Monotonic counter 1 privilege protection

fields.name: BKPRWPRIV
fields.description: Backup registers zone 1 privilege protection

fields.name: BKPWPRIV
fields.description: Backup registers zone 2 privilege protection

fields.name: TAMPPRIV
fields.description: Tamper privilege protection (excluding backup registers) Note: Refer to for details on the read protection.

fields.name: COUNT
fields.description: This register is read-only only and is incremented by one when a write access is done to this register. This register cannot roll-over and is frozen when reaching the maximum value.

fields.name: ERCFG0
fields.description: Configurable device secrets configuration

fields.name: BKP
fields.description: The application can write or read data to and from these registers. In the default (ERASE) configuration this register is reset on a tamper detection event. It is forced to reset value as long as there is at least one internal or external tamper flag being set. This register is also reset when the readout protection (RDP) is disabled.
